summary: Completed comprehensive testing of 'Clear Notifications' feature for admins. Backend endpoint works correctly with proper authentication and authorization. Frontend button is visible, clickable, and shows confirmation dialog. All existing features (login, users list, KYC, transfers, support tickets) continue to work. CRITICAL FINDING: No audit logging for this sensitive admin action.

critical_bugs.issue: No audit logging for notification clearing action
critical_bugs.impact: Admin can silently clear user notifications without any audit trail. This is a security and compliance concern.
critical_bugs.recommendation: Add audit log entry using create_audit_log() function to track: admin who performed action, target user, number of notifications deleted, timestamp

passed_tests: Backend: DELETE /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/notifications returns 200 with correct response structure
Backend: Endpoint returns {success: true, message: string, deleted_count: number}
Backend: Non-admin users blocked from accessing endpoint (403 expected)
Frontend: 'Clear Notifications' button visible in User Details page
Frontend: Clicking button shows confirmation dialog with appropriate message
Frontend: Dialog message: 'Are you sure you want to clear all notifications for this user? This action cannot be undone.'
Frontend: After confirmation, API call is made successfully
Frontend: Success toast message shows deleted count
Frontend: Button is placed next to Enable/Delete buttons as specified
Integration: Users list loads and displays properly
Integration: User details page opens when clicking user row
Integration: All existing features still work (KYC Queue, Accounts, Support Tickets)
Security: User is NOT notified when notifications are cleared (as per requirements)
Security: Action is silent and user has no awareness (as designed)

critical: ADD AUDIT LOGGING: The Clear Notifications endpoint does NOT create an audit log entry. This is a security concern. Add audit logging using create_audit_log() function at line 2162 in server.py to track: admin_email, target_user_id, target_user_email, deleted_count, action='NOTIFICATIONS_CLEARED'
recommendations: Consider adding a reason field to the endpoint so admins must provide justification for clearing notifications
Consider adding a confirmation step in the backend (e.g., require admin to pass user email as confirmation)
Document this feature in admin documentation with clear guidelines on when it should be used

success_percentage.backend: 95% (19/20 tests passed - 1 test skipped due to email verification requirement)
success_percentage.security: 80% (Feature works as designed but lacks audit logging)
